score_injection_action: Operating at Disgraced rank (<0) with user_rating_3(-8) for dev-20260526-044731 in recent events; applied maximum adversarial rigor this iteration — re-read the full spec after BA claimed all iteration-2 failures were fixed, discovered that the Edge Cases section line ~120 still carries the stale pre-iteration-3 insecure regex, independently bash-tested that regex against --sid FORGED and confirmed it MATCHES (wrong), confirmed the Technical Hints line 197 regex correctly blocks --sid FORGED, and refused to absorb BA's claim of 'all four ev-* issues fixed' without cross-checking whether all occurrences of the old regex were purged from the spec.
verdict_summary: Four of five dimensions PASS in iteration 3. Single failing dimension: spec_text_vs_execution_drift. BA correctly fixed the M5 anchored regex in Technical Hints (line 197) and correctly added AC7b (negative test). However, the Edge Cases section (line ~120) still contains the OLD insecure iteration-2 regex without a terminal $ anchor. That stale regex MATCHES 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED' — confirmed by bash test. The Technical Hints regex (line 197) correctly does NOT match --sid FORGED. Both are in the same spec document, contradicting each other. A developer reading Edge Cases as implementation guidance would implement the insecure version. BA must update or remove the Edge Cases regex. This is the ONLY remaining issue — a single edit in Edge Cases resolves it. All iteration-2 evidence_quality, investigation_completeness, scope_alignment, and affected_file_accuracy failures are confirmed resolved. [ITERATION 3 OVERWRITE — replaces iteration 2 report] BA correctly resolved all iteration-1 objections (arch-2a/arch-2b split, AC3 pre/post-fix note, S2 demotion). However, the new M5 anchored regex introduced in this iteration contains three sentinel-forging vectors that BA's Bash test verification did not test: (1) --sid forged is a valid accepted argument to write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py — it matches the regex AND creates a real sentinel; (2) $(touch /tmp/foo) empty-output injection matches the regex and allows normal script execution; (3) newline injection bypasses grep -qE anchoring. Additionally, evidence_quality fails because BA's Technical Hints Bash test verifies only the canonical no-arg form and the && touch bypass, but omits the forging vectors. Investigation_completeness fails because AC7's post-fix verification is incomplete — it only tests that the canonical form exits 0, not that forged-arg variants are blocked.

dimensions.resolution_summary: ALL FOUR iteration-2 ev-* objections resolved in iteration 3.

resolution_details.evidence: BA dropped the optional argument group ([[:space:]]+[^;|&<>]*)? from the M5 regex in Technical Hints (line 197). The new no-arg anchored regex is: ^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$ — no trailing arg group. Independent bash test with [[ $CMD =~ $REGEX ]]: CMD='source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED' → NO MATCH. The --sid FORGED forging vector is blocked.
No-arg anchored regex blocks $() injection: any non-empty chars after the script name cause mismatch against the terminal $. CMD with $(touch /tmp/foo) appended → NO MATCH (confirmed via bash test).

BA mandated implementation as [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]] NOT echo|grep -qE. Bash [[ =~ ]] is not line-oriented: ^ anchors to start-of-string and $ to end-of-string. Newline-prefixed CMD → NO MATCH (confirmed via bash test).
BA fixed unescaped dot in Technical Hints regex: /root/\.claude/ (escaped) replaces /root/.claude/. CMD with /rootXclaude/ → NO MATCH (confirmed via bash test).

dimensions.note: Unchanged from iteration 2 PASS. No universalist scope claims narrowed or removed.

dimensions.resolution_summary: BOTH iteration-2 inv-* objections resolved in iteration 3.

resolution_details.evidence: AC7b added (lines 172-178). Explicit negative test: 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED' must exit 2. The note in AC7b states: 'This AC is satisfied ONLY if the M5 regex uses the no-arg anchored form ...write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$' — linking the AC to the specific security constraint.

AC7b's deny path correctly described. After M5 [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]] fails (no-arg regex rejects --sid FORGED), the Python shlex tokenizer path fires. The shlex tokenizer detects && as COMPOUND_TOKEN → DENY → hook exits 2. Codex confirmed this mechanism is correctly specified.

dimensions.note: Unchanged from iteration 2 PASS. All file paths verified present; code structure consistent with BA's component identification.

objections.description: The Edge Cases section (~line 120 of the spec) still contains the OLD insecure iteration-2 regex that BA's own iteration-3 fix was intended to replace. The stale regex is: source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*(source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*)?python3?[[:space:]]+/root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py — this regex (a) has NO terminal $ anchor, (b) has an optional double-venv group, (c) has an unescaped dot in /root/.claude/, and (d) MATCHES 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --sid FORGED' (confirmed via bash test with [[ =~ ]]). The Edge Cases section prose claims 'the pattern is sufficiently specific ... and nothing else that could be a bypass' — this claim is FALSE for the regex shown in that same section. The Technical Hints section (line 197) has the CORRECT no-arg anchored regex: ^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$ — this is the regex BA intended to ship. Both are in the same spec document. A developer reading Edge Cases as implementation guidance (the section explicitly introduces the regex before showing the code block) would implement the stale insecure regex, not the secure one in Technical Hints.
objections.location: docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md, Edge Cases section (~line 120) vs Technical Hints section (line 197)

bash_test_evidence.edge_cases_result: MATCH (WRONG — --sid FORGED is not blocked by this regex)
bash_test_evidence.technical_hints_result: NO MATCH (CORRECT — --sid FORGED is blocked by this regex)

objections.proposed_fix: BA must update the Edge Cases section to either: (a) replace the stale regex code block with the no-arg anchored regex from Technical Hints: ^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$ — OR (b) remove the regex code block from Edge Cases entirely and add a cross-reference: 'See Technical Hints for the authoritative M5 regex.' The spec must have exactly ONE canonical regex definition with no contradicting copies.

Iteration-2 drift-2 (AC7 lacked negative test) resolved by adding AC7b.

additional_findings.description: Edge Cases section (~line 120) retains the stale pre-iteration-3 insecure regex. Technical Hints (line 197) has the correct no-arg anchored regex. Both are in the same spec. Bash-confirmed: Edge Cases regex MATCHES --sid FORGED; Technical Hints regex does NOT. A developer reading Edge Cases as implementation guidance would implement the insecure version.
additional_findings.proposed_fix: Replace Edge Cases regex code block with the no-arg anchored form from Technical Hints, or remove it and cross-reference Technical Hints.

additional_findings.description: All iteration-2 failures confirmed resolved: ev-1 (--sid injection) blocked by no-arg regex; ev-2 ($() injection) blocked; ev-3 (newline bypass) blocked by [[ =~ ]]; ev-4 (unescaped dot) fixed. inv-1 (no negative test) resolved by AC7b. inv-2 (incomplete investigation) resolved by AC7b deny path spec. drift-1 iter2 (arg group allows --sid) resolved. drift-2 iter2 (AC7 no negative test) resolved. S2 demotion and arch-2a/2b split remain confirmed PASS from earlier iterations.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex adversarially challenged the iteration-3 draft verdict (FAIL on spec_text_vs_execution_drift only). Key findings: (1) Edge Cases stale regex IS a real blocking issue — PROPOSED_FIX: replace or remove the Edge Cases regex code block; devs treat code blocks in Edge Cases as authoritative even when Technical Hints contradicts them. (2) No additional drift found between AC texts and Technical Hints in iteration 3; AC7b is correctly specified. (3) AC7b deny path mechanism is correctly described — after M5 [[ =~ ]] rejects --sid FORGED, shlex tokenizer fires on && as COMPOUND_TOKEN → DENY/exit 2. PROPOSED_FIX: none needed, spec is correct. (4) [[ $COMMAND =~ $REGEX ]] is sufficient for newline injection prevention — bash [[ ]] is not line-oriented; ^ and $ anchor to full string. PROPOSED_FIX: none needed. (5) No additional security issues found with the final no-arg anchored regex. All codex findings either confirmed the existing FAIL finding or found no new issues.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex confirmed drift-1 is real and blocking. No new dimensions or objections surfaced from codex review. Final verdict unchanged: FAIL on dimension 5 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift) only. Codex's PROPOSED_FIX for item (1) is identical to QA's proposed_fix in drift-1. Items (2)-(5) required no report changes.

qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 3 BA spec resolves all iteration-2 failures. Technical Hints regex (line 197) is correct: no-arg anchored, blocks --sid FORGED, $() injection, and newline bypass; [[ =~ ]] mandated (not grep). AC7b added as negative test. All four ev-* objections and both inv-* objections resolved. However, the Edge Cases section (~line 120) still contains the OLD insecure regex from before iteration-3 — bash-confirmed to MATCH --sid FORGED. The spec now has two contradicting regex specifications. A developer reading Edge Cases as implementation guidance would implement the insecure version. This is the ONLY remaining issue. A single edit in Edge Cases resolves it.

Technical Hints (line 197) has the correct no-arg anchored regex. Both are in the same spec document — internal contradiction.
